rulings.Case Title: ALICE TORRES VS. CHASE MANHATTAN BANK USA, N.A.
rulings.Rulings: DEMURRER - SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD INDIVIDUAL ACTION PER CC 1788.30(a). OVERRULE AS TO 2ND & 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION. PARARGRAPH 14(e) OF THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT SEEKS ATTORNEY'S FEES EVEN THOUGH THE AGREEMENT DID NOT AUTHORIZE THEM. SUCH CONDUCT IS AN UNLAWFUL, UNFAIR, AND DECEPTIVE BUSINESS PRACTICE. WHILE THE PARTIES DO NOT DISPUTE THAT ALL OF THE CARDHOLDER AGREEMENTS CONTAIN CHOICE OF LAW CLAUSES, IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER THE PARTIES DISPUTE WHETHER ALL OF THE CARDHOLDER AGREEMENTS CONTAIN ATTORNEY'S FEES CLAUSES. IF THERE IS A DISPUTE, IT IS IMPROPER TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE CARDHOLDER AGREEMENT WITH REGARD TO THE ATTORNEY'S FEES ISSUE. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE NOT NECESSARILY SUBJECT TO DELAWARE LAW. DEFENDANT FAILS TO HIGHLIGHT THE APPLICABLE DELAWARE LAW AND THEREFORE, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER DELAWARE'S LAW IS CONTRARY TO A FUNDAMENTAL POLICY OF CALIFORNIA. EVEN THOUGH PLAINTIFF MAY LACK STANDING UNDER CC 1788 ET SEQ, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT NECESSARILY LACK STANDING TO BRING A UPA CAUSE OF ACTION PREDICATED UPON CC 1788. See Stop Youth Addiction v. Lucky Stores, Inc., (1998) 17 Cal.4th 553. (PB)

rulings.Case Title: ALICE TORRES VS. CHASE MANHATTAN BANK USA, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Certain Portions Of The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: GRANT WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. ALLEGATIONS NOT NECESSARILY TIME BARRED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF APPEARS TO WANT TO PLEAD VIOLATION OF 1788.13(e) AS THE PREDICATE STATUTE FOR A UPA CAUSE OF ACTION. NO SHOWING THAT CC 1788.13(e) WAS RECENTLY AMENDED. (PB)
